name: Tom Malinowski
subject: Notes From Tom
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Districts for Democracy
body:
| | Dear friends, Tuesday is election day in New Jersey. And this year, one of the most important ballots we will cast will be for school board.

Did you know that the race in your school district may be one of the most contested in the state this year? [Please click here for a guide to candidates](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/72811423/436455426/157604416?refcode=EM_NBI_DFD_20231104_ST_notefromTom_0_CST_Tom_na_na_na&refcode2=325b7b6e-687b-ee11-b004-00224832eb73&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzYzOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzMjViN2I2ZS02ODdiLWVlMTEtYjAwNC0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=nOSVJkpl3zN_HSKulZcqy6iy9hdxFYe7o315h5oGN1Q=&emci=aa3ac178-6a7a-ee11-b004-00224832eb73&emdi=325b7b6e-687b-ee11-b004-00224832eb73&ceid=19214294), and read on to learn what’s at stake.

School board elections are non-partisan, and aren’t supposed to be controversial. But across America, extreme right wing groups have been trying to take over school boards to censor what our kids read and learn. I’m sure you’ve seen the headlines from states like Florida that are banning books about civil rights heroes and the Holocaust and even Shakespeare plays, not to mention any book that might have an LGBT character.

Some people are surprised to hear that this is happening in New Jersey, too.

MAGA-backed candidates have taken over school boards in places like Bernards Township, where they [banned a high school sociology textbook](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/72811424/436455427/1774648265?refcode=EM_NBI_DFD_20231104_ST_notefromTom_0_CST_Tom_na_na_na&refcode2=325b7b6e-687b-ee11-b004-00224832eb73&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzYzOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzMjViN2I2ZS02ODdiLWVlMTEtYjAwNC0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=nOSVJkpl3zN_HSKulZcqy6iy9hdxFYe7o315h5oGN1Q=&emci=aa3ac178-6a7a-ee11-b004-00224832eb73&emdi=325b7b6e-687b-ee11-b004-00224832eb73&ceid=19214294), and in Hanover, where they’ve pushed [a policy of having school officials to out LGBT students to their families](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/72811425/436455428/1227580828?refcode=EM_NBI_DFD_20231104_ST_notefromTom_0_CST_Tom_na_na_na&refcode2=325b7b6e-687b-ee11-b004-00224832eb73&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzYzOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzMjViN2I2ZS02ODdiLWVlMTEtYjAwNC0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=nOSVJkpl3zN_HSKulZcqy6iy9hdxFYe7o315h5oGN1Q=&emci=aa3ac178-6a7a-ee11-b004-00224832eb73&emdi=325b7b6e-687b-ee11-b004-00224832eb73&ceid=19214294), even when that might put the kids at risk. In one Bergen County district, a [MAGA-majority board eliminated all funding for student mental health programs](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/72811426/436455429/-729736050?refcode=EM_NBI_DFD_20231104_ST_notefromTom_0_CST_Tom_na_na_na&refcode2=325b7b6e-687b-ee11-b004-00224832eb73&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzYzOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzMjViN2I2ZS02ODdiLWVlMTEtYjAwNC0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=nOSVJkpl3zN_HSKulZcqy6iy9hdxFYe7o315h5oGN1Q=&emci=aa3ac178-6a7a-ee11-b004-00224832eb73&emdi=325b7b6e-687b-ee11-b004-00224832eb73&ceid=19214294#:~:text=NJ%20Spotlight%20News-,Ramapo%20Indian%20Hills%20school%20board%20ends%20mental%20health%20programs,board%20member%20missing%20the%20vote./), because letting kids talk to counselors in confidence violated the school board members’ notion of “parental rights.”

**New Jersey has the best public schools in America.** Board members should stay focused on educational excellence, not get dragged into our nation’s partisan culture wars. They should keep teaching our kids to think critically and to distinguish facts from lies, and remain a safe and welcoming place for children of all backgrounds and needs.

The last thing we need is for teachers to censor themselves in class for fear of offending loud groups of political extremists, or for our school districts to spend our tax dollars defending themselves from the lawsuits that these culture war controversies inevitably bring. | | **[Our 2023 BOE Voter Guide](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/72811427/436455430/157604416?refcode=EM_NBI_DFD_20231104_ST_notefromTom_0_CST_Tom_na_na_na&refcode2=325b7b6e-687b-ee11-b004-00224832eb73&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzYzOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzMjViN2I2ZS02ODdiLWVlMTEtYjAwNC0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=nOSVJkpl3zN_HSKulZcqy6iy9hdxFYe7o315h5oGN1Q=&emci=aa3ac178-6a7a-ee11-b004-00224832eb73&emdi=325b7b6e-687b-ee11-b004-00224832eb73&ceid=19214294)**
---
Our voter guide highlights those New Jersey races — including potentially yours — where a school board could flip toward, or away from, an extreme MAGA majority this year, and lists the candidates who share our values.

I don’t know if these candidates are registered as Democrats, Republicans, or unaffiliated, and I don’t care. But you can be certain that they won’t ban books, or censor classroom discussions, or persecute kids who are different. They will support our public schools and work hard to make them even better. I hope you’ll support them!

So please take a look at the guide. Share it with neighbors and friends who may not know where their school board candidates stand on the issues. And don’t forget to vote — either in person on Tuesday, or by turning in your Vote-by-Mail ballot before then!

Thank you for all you do for our democracy.

Tom Malinowski
* * *
| Districts for Democracy will work one school board, legislative, and congressional district at a time to recruit and support candidates committed to freedom of inquiry, thought, and speech for everyone. We want to keep the culture wars out of our schools, return school board elections to their nonpartisan roots, and keep our schools and universities focused on their central mission: helping our kids become educated and engaged citizens of these United States.

clean_body: | | Dear friends, Tuesday is election day in New Jersey. And this year, one of the most important ballots we will cast will be for school board. Did you know that the race in your school district may be one of the most contested in the state this year? , and read on to learn what’s at stake. School board elections are non-partisan, and aren’t supposed to be controversial. But across America, extreme right wing groups have been trying to take over school boards to censor what our kids read and learn. I’m sure you’ve seen the headlines from states like Florida that are banning books about civil rights heroes and the Holocaust and even Shakespeare plays, not to mention any book that might have an LGBT character. Some people are surprised to hear that this is happening in New Jersey, too. MAGA-backed candidates have taken over school boards in places like Bernards Township, where they , and in Hanover, where they’ve pushed , even when that might put the kids at risk. In one Bergen County district, a , because letting kids talk to counselors in confidence violated the school board members’ notion of “parental rights.” New Jersey has the best public schools in America. Board members should stay focused on educational excellence, not get dragged into our nation’s partisan culture wars. They should keep teaching our kids to think critically and to distinguish facts from lies, and remain a safe and welcoming place for children of all backgrounds and needs. The last thing we need is for teachers to censor themselves in class for fear of offending loud groups of political extremists, or for our school districts to spend our tax dollars defending themselves from the lawsuits that these culture war controversies inevitably bring. | | Our voter guide highlights those New Jersey races — including potentially yours — where a school board could flip toward, or away from, an extreme MAGA majority this year, and lists the candidates who share our values.
